problem_statement: Customers experience long waiting times due to full queues when arriving at Moo Kata restaurants without prior booking, leading to frustration and potential loss of business.
solution_overview: Develop a web-based table reservation system that allows customers to view real-time queue status, make advance table bookings, and pay a deposit online. The system aims to enhance customer experience by reducing wait times and ensuring service availability even during peak periods.
functional_requirements: Browse Restaurants and View Table Availability
Table Reservation Functionality (select date, time, number of guests)
Booking Confirmation and Notifications (e.g., email, SMS)
Restaurant Dashboard for managing table availability, bookings, and queue (implied for full system operation)
non_functional_requirements: Scalability: The system must be able to handle a high volume of concurrent users and transactions, especially during peak seasons and festivals, without performance degradation.
Reliability: The system must maintain high availability and stability, preventing crashes or service interruptions even under heavy load.
Performance: Real-time queue updates and booking processes should be fast and responsive to user interactions.
Security: All user data and payment information must be securely handled, adhering to industry best practices and data protection regulations.
Usability: The user interface should be intuitive and easy to navigate for all customer segments.
tech_stack_recommendation: Backend: Node.js (with Express.js or NestJS) for high concurrency and real-time capabilities via WebSockets, or Python (with FastAPI/Django) for robust API development.
Frontend: React, Vue.js, or Angular for a dynamic, responsive, and rich user interface.
Database: PostgreSQL for reliable transactional data, complemented by Redis for caching and real-time data handling (e.g., queue updates).
Cloud Platform: AWS, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), or Microsoft Azure for scalable and managed infrastructure services (e.g., load balancers, auto-scaling groups, managed databases).
Real-time Communication: WebSockets (e.g., Socket.IO) for instant queue status updates and notifications.
Payment Gateway: Integration with a reputable third-party payment gateway (e.g., Stripe, Omise, 2C2P) for secure online deposit processing.
